citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
In response to an internal complaint representatives from the IACUC oversaw procedures conducted for Protocol #RA006
on August 20th and August 27th-28th. The ACUC observed the research staff care and use of 5 study rabbits. During
their observations, the IACUC noted numerous deviations from the procedures outlined in the approved protocol. The
deviations are as follows:
1. Syringes and catheters were reused at least twice per animal. The protocol indicates single use of syringes and
catheters.
2. A cat bag was used to restrain the rabbits. The protocol describes the use of the “burrito” method for rabbit restraint.
3. No record of isoflurane induction. The protocol indicates the rabbits will be induced with isoflurane prior to the
administration of ketamine/xylazine for anesthesia.
5. The ketamine/xylazine was administered intravenously as a bolus. The protocol indicates the drug combination should
be administered intravenously by continuous infusion.
6. Research staff performed the resuscitation procedure while the rabbits were conscious. The protocol indicates the
procedure should be performed under anesthesia.
7. Research staff did not consistently use aseptic techniques to clean the VAB ports. The staff acknowledged that the
protocol indicates the VAB ports should be cleaned with 70% alcohol prior to syringe connection for blood collection and
agent administration.
Numerous procedures were performed by the research staff in a manner not outlined in the protocol. The research staff
continues to have protocol compliance issues following corrective actions implemented by the IACUC in April and August
2024.
Procedures in an IACUC approved protocol must be followed to ensure animals do not experience unnecessary pain or
distress and to ensure processes are conducted in a manner consistent with industry standards. Proposed activities for
animal use that are approved by the IACUC should be followed or further appropriate review and approval would be
needed in accordance with 2.31(d) of Subpart C. Correction: The IACUC assigned another principal investigator to
Protocol #RA006 to implement a corrective action plan.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
After a discussion with lab members and procedural observations related to Protocol #RA006, the IACUC noted several
instances of non-compliance that may have resulted in unnecessary pain and distress:
During IACUC observations of the hemorrhage procedure on August 20, 2025, two rabbits were anesthetized with
Ketamine/Xylazine (IM). Both animals roused and regained consciousness approximately 30 minutes after the beginning
of the procedure. Animals were righting themselves from a side lying position to sitting up and moving around in the rabbit
bin. One animal attempted to hop out of the bin several times. Research staff were required to physically restrain the
animals while still manually pulling blood for the hemorrhage. Animals appeared to intermittently lose and regain
consciousness (becoming very active) as the hemorrhage procedure continued.
During observations on August 28, 2026, a rabbit was redosed 4 times to maintain sedation. The animal roused quickly
and was alert after each dose wore off. Additionally, IACUC noticed the telemetry device readings did not change to
indicate the rabbit was awakening. The monitoring parameters of the telemetry device is not effective in determining the
rabbit is coming out of a plane of anesthesia.
Proposed activities must include procedures that will avoid or minimize discomfort, distress, and pain to animals.
Correction: The IACUC required the research staff to be retrained to address these non-compliances. Staff received
training on 09/2025 and 01/2026 for Protocol #RA006.

citations.desc: Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC).
citations.narrative:
1. The endpoint criteria in Protocol #RA006, relies on set parameters for Mean Arterial Pressure (MAP) that will remotely
trigger an alert to the principal investigator if the readings for MAP reaches a certain percentage of the normal range
(70mm Hg). However, conflicting low-pressure parameters are noted in the protocol - MAP less than 40mm Hg versus
less than 35mm Hg (50% less then normal).
2. Protocol #RA006 indicates that once a trigger message from the telemetry device is received a member of the research
staff will be present to determine if vital signs meet the endpoint criteria. Research staff indicated that the trigger
messages is sent when the vital signs are in euthanasia range and they are allowed 30 minutes to arrive at the facility to
evaluate and/ or euthanize the rabbit. Animals may be in pain or distress for up to an hour. The expectation as outlined in
the protocol is that the rabbits would be euthanized when the endpoint criteria is met. The IACUC concluded that allowing
the animal to be maintained longer than 30 minutes increases the likelihood that death would be the endpoint. The intent
of the endpoint parameters is to intervene by euthanasia.
3. An IACUC review of telemetry data from Protocol #RA006 for one animal record showed the MAP spiked for a period of
15-20 minutes before rapidly declining. The animal died within "' 4 minutes of the decline.
Additionally, during the USDA inspection, records were reviewed for three rabbits that were found dead. The telemetry
data for two rabbits showed a sharp drop in MAP below the established MAP endpoint parameter and the animals died
within 3 minutes of the sudden change. The MAP for one rabbit dropped below the established parameter continuously for
a period of approximately 45 minutes before the animal died. The data suggests these rabbits may have experienced
unrelieved pain or distress.
The MAP trigger parameter and the process for intervention is not effective in ensuring distress and pain is relieved in a
timely manner. Proposed activities in which animals may experience severe or chronic distress or pain that cannot be
relieved should be painlessly euthanized when endpoint criteria are met. Correction: The IACUC approved amendments
to Protocol #RA006 to address this issue on December 17, 2025.

2.32(b) During an IACUC oversight reviews of Protocol #RA006 in August 2025, it was noted that the research staff,
including the trainers, were unfamiliar with the procedures as outlined in protocol. Research staff performing procedures
asked the IACUC observers questions that indicated they did not know procedural details that were clearly stated in the
protocol:
1. Research staff asked if the protocol indicated that rabbits should be sedated with xylazine/ketamine prior to euthanasia.
2. Research staff asked if Cathflo could be administered a second time after the waiting period or if the animal needed to
be euthanized.
After discussions with consulting staff and the principal investigator, and the lab staff, the IACUC concluded that the
persons providing training were unfamiliar with the details of the protocol and not knowledgeable of the processes and
procedures as outlined in the protocol.
Also, during the IACUC review, only one lab member received the training established in 2024 as part of a corrective
action plan for previous training deficiencies. Records indicate new lab members did not receive the same training.
Additionally, the IACUC noted that surgery and experimental records were incomplete with missing documentation of
dates for procedures, anesthesia doses, blood pressure measurements. Some forms did not have signatures and dates
indicating the record was reviewed as required as part of a corrective action plan in 2024. During the USDA records
review, it was noted that prior to the IACUC observations, many records had very few notes documenting procedures
performed on the rabbits.
The current research team for Protocol #RA006 were not familiar with the processes and procedures outlined in SOPs
and the protocol to adequately perform their study responsibilities. Qualifications of research personnel must be reviewed
with sufficient frequency to fulfill the research facilities responsibilities under this section and section 2.31. Correct by
training all new research staff and conduct training whenever there is an approved protocol amendment. NOTE: The
current research team completed training in January 2026.

During the IACUC observations and discussions for Protocol #RA006, research staff members indicated they did not
aseptically clean the VAB ports prior to connection for blood collection and agent administration. as outlined in the
protocol. Additionally, the syringes and catheters were placed in the rabbit bin with feces and urine. Personnel must be
trained in the importance of sterile techniques to prevent contamination and infection. Correction: The current research
team completed training in January 2026.

August 20, 2025, the IACUC observer informed the research staff that the Protocol #RA006 allows for “top-off doses” of
ketamine/xylazine to maintain unconsciousness during procedures. Research staff indicated they were not aware that the
protocol has provisions to administer additional doses of ketamine/xylazine IM (“top-off” doses). On August 28, 2025, after
reading the protocol, the research staff believed a maximum of 4 top-off doses were allowed. The protocol does not
indicate a minimum or maximum number of doses.
During observations on August 27, 2025, the IACUC noticed that the anesthetic equipment was connected improperly and
showed the research staff members the correct way to set up the equipment.
On August 28, 2025, the veterinarian noticed that the isoflurane level did not reach a maintenance level of anesthesia as
outlined in the protocol and facilitated replacement with a properly working anesthetic machine.
Research staff were not knowledgeable of the proper administration of anesthesia. Continuous review and training of
study processes and procedures is necessary to ensure animals are adequately anesthetized when study activities are
performed. Correction: The current research team completed training in January 2026.
This inspection and exit interview were conducted with the facility representatives.
No study activities were conducted in 2026 to fully assess compliance. Further review will occur at the next inspection.
n

2.31(c)(7)  Repeat
   
Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
In response to an internal complaint representatives from the IACUC oversaw procedures conducted for Protocol #RA006

Institutional Animal Care and Use Committee (IACUC). 
After a discussion with lab members and procedural observations related to Protocol #RA006, the IACUC noted several

Personnel qualifications.
